title: JEECUP Admit Card 2026 Will Be Released on 27 May

detail.title: JEECUP Admit Card 2026 Will Be Released on 27 May
detail.short_info: JEECUP Admit Card 2026 will be released today, 27 May 2026, on the official website jeecup.admissions.nic.in for the UP Polytechnic (UPJEE) exam scheduled from 2 to 9 June 2026. Check today’s admit card release update, exact time, direct download link, step‑by‑step process to get the hall ticket, details mentioned, documents to carry and important exam‑day instructions here.

sections.heading: JEECUP Admit Card 2026 Will Be Released on 27 May

content.text: The Joint Entrance Examination Council, Uttar Pradesh (JEECUP) will release the JEECUP Admit Card 2026 on 27 May 2026 on its official admission portal. Registered candidates will be able to download the UP Polytechnic (UPJEE) hall ticket online from jeecup.admissions.nic.in using their application number and password/date of birth.

As per the revised schedule, the JEECUP 2026 exam will now be conducted from 2 June to 9 June 2026 in computer‑based test (CBT) mode, instead of the earlier dates in May. The admit card download link will be activated on 27 May and will remain available till the respective exam date of each candidate’s group.
Download Here: JEECUP Admit Card 2026

rows: Joint Entrance Examination Council, Uttar Pradesh (JEECUP) – UPJEE (Polytechnic) 2026
Admission to polytechnic/diploma courses in engineering, technology and other streams in participating institutes of Uttar Pradesh
Computer-based test (CBT) in English and Hindi
Admit Card 2026 link available on the official website
Application number and password or date of birth

sections.heading: How to Download JEECUP Admit Card 2026

content.text: Once the admit card is released, candidates can download the JEECUP 2026 hall ticket by following these steps:

items: Visit the official JEECUP portal jeecup.admissions.nic.in.
On the homepage, click on the link “JEECUP Admit Card 2026” / “Download UPJEE (Polytechnic) Admit Card” for your respective group.
Enter your Application Number and Password or Date of Birth, then submit the captcha code.
Click on Sign In / Login to access your dashboard. The JEECUP Admit Card 2026 will appear on the screen.
Check all details printed on the hall ticket, including name, group, exam date, shift and centre address.
Download and take 2-3 clear A4‑size printouts of the admit card for exam day and future reference.

content.text: If the site is slow due to heavy traffic, candidates are advised to try after some time and avoid multiple refresh attempts.

sections.heading: Details Mentioned on JEECUP Admit Card 2026

content.text: The JEECUP 2026 admit card will carry the following important information:

items: Candidate’s name
JEECUP application number / roll number
Group and paper (A, B, C, D, E, F, G, H, I, K, etc.)
Exam date and shift / timing (within 2-9 June 2026)
Examination centre name, city and full address
Candidate’s photograph and signature (if printed)
Category and gender (if applicable)
Reporting time and gate‑closing time
Important exam‑day instructions and guidelines

content.text: If there is any mismatch in your name, photo, group, or exam centre details, you must contact JEECUP helpdesk immediately using the contact details provided on the official website.

sections.heading: Documents Required with JEECUP Admit Card 2026

content.text: On the day of the UPJEE (Polytechnic) 2026 exam, candidates must carry:

items: A printed copy of the JEECUP Admit Card 2026 (preferably a clear A4‑size printout; colour or high‑quality black and white).
One original valid photo ID proof, such as Aadhaar Card, PAN Card, Voter ID, Driving Licence, Passport or any other government‑issued ID accepted by JEECUP.
Recent passport‑size photographs, if mentioned in the detailed instructions (to be safe, carry at least 1-2 photos similar to the application form image).

content.text: Candidates without the admit card and valid photo ID will not be allowed to enter the exam centre under any circumstances.
JEECUP has laid down several exam‑day guidelines for candidates appearing in the UP Polytechnic 2026 exam:

items: Reach the exam centre well before the reporting time mentioned on the admit card; latecomers arriving after gate‑closing time will not be permitted to enter.
Carry only essential items: JEECUP admit card, photo ID, photographs (if required), and basic stationery as allowed; follow the list of permitted and prohibited items mentioned in the instructions.

Do not bring mobile phones, smart watches, calculators (if not expressly permitted), Bluetooth devices, earphones, books, notes or any electronic gadgets into the exam hall; possession of such items may lead to cancellation of candidature.
Follow all instructions displayed on the computer screen and those announced by invigilators; any attempt at unfair means can result in disqualification and further action.
Keep your JEECUP hall ticket safely even after the exam, as it may be required during result, counselling and admission processes.
